intro: Tracheliopsis tubulosa, also known as Tracheliopsis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering various health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Tracheliopsis tubulosa include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and other sulfur-containing compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other ailments.
